rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope
Place implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure during fusion
Insert annex blocks in the appropriate scope based on the contract type
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Preserve the existing subcomponents and connections when adding annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
